untranslatable_reason: Diamond pattern detection requires geometric price pattern recognition (widening then contracting movements) which cannot be expressed using only the allowed indicators and operators. The pattern itself must be pre-identified in the data or calculated externally. Using Donchian channels as a proxy for breakout detection is a simplification that approximates breakout behavior but does not capture the true diamond pattern geometry specified by Bulkowski.
translation_notes: Signal requires explicit diamond pattern identification which is not available in the allowed indicator set. Substituted Donchian upper/lower as a mechanical breakout proxy. True implementation requires pattern detection algorithm. Entry on close above Donchian upper approximates breakout above diamond boundary. Exit on close below Donchian lower captures reversal. Success rate (73% bull) and average rise (36%) noted from source but not encoded as they apply to the true pattern, not the Donchian proxy. Sample size: 288 diamonds analyzed per Bulkowski. Premature breakout rate documented as <12/288 but cannot be modeled with available indicators.
